title: tecoma lepidota
intro: Tecoma lepidota, also known as the yellow bells or Mexican honeysuckle, is a flowering shrub native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Tecoma lepidota include its ability to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its use as an expectorant to relieve coughs and congestion. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to treat a range of health issues.
